steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to specify how many chromosomes the fennec fox has. The correct choice must name the fennec fox and state its chromosome count directly — nothing more, nothing less.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘The fennec fox (*Vulpes zerda*) and the red fox (*Vulpes vulpes*) both have chromosomes, but the fennec fox has more than the red fox does.’: gives only a comparison ("more than") without stating the actual count. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘The fennec fox (*Vulpes zerda*) has sixty-four chromosomes.’: names the fennec fox and directly states its chromosome count. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘The fennec fox's chromosomes contain genes, which are critical to determining an organism's physical traits.’: explains what chromosomes do but never gives the count. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘The red fox, a species of fox, has thirty-four structures called chromosomes.’: gives a chromosome count, but for the red fox, not the fennec fox. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
